Ad Text:
The Department of Education is seeking a Director, Business Solutions and Advice to lead a high-performing advisory function within the Finance, Procurement and Facilities Branch.
This role sits at the centre of complex, high-impact work, partnering with senior leaders across the Department to improve performance, solve critical challenges and support informed decision making.
You will lead a team delivering financial, analytical and business advisory services across a diverse portfolio of initiatives.

About the Department of Education:  

Working for the Queensland Department of Education means joining an organisation that values its people and promotes leadership and innovation. Be part of an environment that respects professionalism and diversity, offers training and development opportunities and embraces flexible careers and work-life balance. Find out more about working with us.  

Key Highlights:

Lead high-impact work across one of Queensland's largest and most complex departments.
Partner directly with senior leaders to shape decisions and drive improvement.
Deliver outcomes that move beyond analysis into implementation and impact.

About You:

You bring a background in consulting, advisory, audit or business improvement and are comfortable operating in complex environments.

You will demonstrate:

Strong stakeholder engagement and influencing capability at a senior level.
The ability to navigate ambiguity and translate insight into action.
A track record of delivering outcomes, not just recommendations.
Sound judgement and decision-making capability.
Experience leading and developing high-performing teams.
Experience within government or large organisations is highly regarded.

Why Join:

This role offers the opportunity to work on meaningful, system-level challenges within a purpose-driven environment. You will gain broad exposure across financial performance, operational improvement and strategic initiatives while contributing to outcomes that have a tangible and lasting impact.

How to Apply:

Attach a brief resume including contact details for 2 referees (referees should have an understanding of your relevant work history) ;
Attach a 2-page written statement in response to the attached role description.  

To be considered, please click apply and you will be redirected to OnTalent recruitment agency.  For a confidential discussion, contact: Ashleigh McMillan | 07 3305 5800.  

We welcome all applicants to share any support needed to ensure our recruitment process is inclusive.  

Applications remain current for 12 months from the closing date and may be considered for appointment to identical or similar vacancies within the Department.  

Applications from recruitment agencies will not be accepted.

Job Description


We are looking for an Agentic Transformation Lead to join our Product Operations team!  

This exciting and newly created role is responsible for supporting the embedment of the strategic adoption of AI technologies, design and management of Agentic AI agents in the future state Product Operations operating model. 

The role focuses on bridging the technologies into a digital workforce where a team of product operations associates manage Agentic AI Agents. You’ll also work on the design of workflows and agentic AI build and support continuous process improvements, optimising workflows and key business processes to drive the strategic design of the agentic human work system. 

You’ll also make an impact by: 

The role of the Agentic Transformation Lead is to design, implement and drive the optimisation and embedment of AI technologies through: 

Strategy and implementation support for the creation of a digital workforce design blueprint  
Analysis of current and future business processes design using change management principles aligned to the product operations goals to drive efficiencies 
Executing the redesign of manual processes to enhance operational efficiency and productivity through the relevant AI technologies following Proof of Concept (POCs)  
Supporting project initiatives to leverage both Agentic and Robotics for cost efficiencies in the workflow design using process intelligence technologies 
Consider the change impacts to human in the loop design principles to ensure risk controls are considered 
Work with the strategic direction of Cuscal’s enterprise AI strategy, policies and frameworks 
Ensure adherence to process governance framework within Product Operations
Assist with the implementation of operational inefficiencies in existing business processes, improving their effectiveness, enhance the workflows, and optimise performance 
Assist with the analysis, design and working through redesign and reengineering practices using six sigma methodologies to optimise processes to drive efficiency 

Qualifications


What you’ll bring 

You thrive in ambiguity and can solve complex problems. You’ve got excellent communication skills and work productively in a high-pressure environment and are keen to contribute to a dynamic team.

To succeed in this role, you’ll have: 

Implementation of AI to improve workforce design and processes 
Exposure to operations, automation, or customer related functions within financial services  
Strong analytical skills, quickly understanding complex concepts and translating them into actionable insights  
Relevant experience in project and change management analysis, data performance and or business analysis, process improvement, or data analysis  
Experience working in Agile, and knowledge of JIRA  
Understanding of ways of working in scrum 
Proven experience in people leadership and driving high performing teams 
Understanding of Cyber security and IT environments 
Incident management protocols and procedural knowledge (including Business Continuity) 
Strategic thinking and proficiency in change management of behavioural shifts in the workplace 
Workplace & IT policies and frameworks 
Understanding of Microsoft Office Suite, JIRA, Confluence 
Management of people, and delivery of initiatives using industry frameworks (change management and project delivery) 
Sound knowledge of business process modelling and workflow documentation

The role

Passionate about leading high‑impact machine learning initiatives and delivering scalable, production‑ready AI solutions?

Motivated to mentor and grow a team of ML and data engineers while remaining hands‑on with advanced model development and deployment?

Inspired to join a collaborative Data & AI organisation that values innovation, technical excellence, and continuous learning?

As our new Machine Learning Engineer – Team Lead, you will play a pivotal role in leading the design and delivery of machine learning solutions end‑to‑end, while providing technical leadership and mentorship to a small, high‑performing engineering team. You will bridge business objectives with advanced ML techniques, guide architectural decisions, and ensure models are production‑ready, scalable, and continuously improved.

Key responsibilities will include:

Lead and mentor a team of ML and Data Engineers, providing technical guidance, regular feedback, and supporting professional development.
Own the end‑to‑end lifecycle of machine learning initiatives, translating business requirements into well‑defined ML problem statements, technical solutions, and delivery plans.
Design, build, train, and evaluate advanced machine learning models, and contribute to architectural decisions for scalable ML pipelines and cloud‑based deployments (Azure preferred).
Partner with MLOps and DevOps teams to implement CI/CD pipelines, model versioning, monitoring, retraining, and production support best practices.
Collaborate cross‑functionally with Data Engineering, Software Development, Sales & Pre‑Sales, and leadership stakeholders, while staying current with emerging ML technologies and driving adoption where valuable.

What we need to see from you
Proven experience delivering machine learning solutions into production environments.
Strong technical expertise in Python and ML frameworks such as TensorFlow, PyTorch, and Scikit‑learn.
Solid understanding of end‑to‑end ML pipelines, data preprocessing, feature engineering, model evaluation, and monitoring.
Experience working with cloud platforms (Azure preferred), Docker, Kubernetes, and MLOps tooling (e.g., MLflow, Airflow).
Ability to balance technical depth with leadership responsibilities and commercial awareness and having experience working within agile delivery environments and across multiple stakeholder groups

Job Function

Software & Cloud
